reasoning: The Israel-Iran ceasefire was implemented in late June 2025 after a major conflict. As of October 26, 2025, searches indicate the ceasefire remains intact between the two countries. Furthermore, Israel and Hamas reached a ceasefire on October 10, 2025, indicating a broader regional de-escalation trend. With only 5 days remaining until the October 31 deadline, the probability of a fresh unintercepted missile or air strike specifically between Israel and Iran is highly unlikely. Because Polymarket options with 5 days until expiration tend to trade very low (likely under 10%) when no precipitating events are occurring, I estimate an outside probability around 9%.
